summary: Matched a large cluster of previously stubbed functions in src/melee/ft/ft_0D14.c, mainly throw, appeal, smash-charge, and C-stick input helper logic. The bot report credited 28 new ft_0D14 matches and +2956 matched bytes overall, with ftCo_AppealS_IASA improved to 98.55%. Supporting header and type work exposed ftCo_8008E5A4, added fighterthrow motion vars, refined SmashAttr and ftCommonData field types, and typed the Fighter_804D6528 global table used by smash-charge visual shifts.

key_files.why: Main matching work: replaced many /// # stubs with C implementations for ftCo_ThrowB_Phys, ftCo_ThrownHi_Cam, fn_800DE798, ftCo_AppealS_Anim, ftCo_AppealS_IASA, smash-charge helpers, and numerous C-stick threshold predicates.

Updated prototypes to match new implementations, including fn_800DE798, ftCo_800DEE84 taking Fighter_GObj*, ftCo_800DEEB8 returning float, ftCo_800DEEE8 returning Vec2*, and several bool C-stick helpers.
Retyped ftCommonData offsets x7C8/x7F4/x7F8/x7FC as floats and changed SmashAttr x212C/x212D from s8 to u8, enabling correct indexing and threshold comparisons.
Added mv.co.fighterthrow layout with victim, self_vel_y, and self_vel_x fields used by throw physics/collision callbacks.
Promoted Fighter_804D6528 from UNK_T to a structured table with Vec2* x0 and int x4, used by ftCo_800DEEE8 and ftCo_800DF0D0.
Matched the new typed declaration for Fighter_804D6528.
Adjusted ftAction_80073008 argument extraction/call to ftCo_800DEE84, though the bot reported a small match regression in this function.
Made ftCo_8008E5A4 externally declared instead of only static-forward-declared in the C file.
Added semantic comments identifying gm_8016B0D4 as friendly fire and gm_8016B0FC as single-button mode, which matters for C-stick gating.

what_changed: Implemented 28 newly matching functions in main/melee/ft/ft_0D14 according to the decomp bot report.
Added ftCo_ThrowB_Phys with separate air/ground behavior: air throw can restore stored self velocity from mv.co.fighterthrow, otherwise calls ft_80085134; grounded calls ft_80085004.
Implemented ftCo_ThrownHi_Cam with a Kirby-specific camera path when the victim is Kirby in ftCo_MS_ThrowHi, otherwise using ftCamera_UpdateCameraBox.
Implemented fn_800DE798 as a small collision helper calling ftColl_8007B8CC with the fighter and mv.co.fighterthrow.victim.
Implemented ftCo_AppealS_Anim to exit through ft_8008A2BC when no animation frames remain.
Implemented most of ftCo_AppealS_IASA input-interrupt chain; bot report says it improved from 0.00% to 98.55%, not fully matched.
Implemented smash-charge setup, damage multiplier, shift lookup, frame/SFX progression, and release handling helpers: ftCo_800DEE84, ftCo_800DEEB8, ftCo_800DEEE8, ftCo_800DEF38, and ftCo_800DF0D0.

Implemented C-stick threshold/helper functions ftCo_800DF1C8 through ftCo_800DF8E8 using current vs previous cstick values, p_ftCommonData thresholds, ABS/SQ, facing_dir, and single-button/item gating.
Added a local static inline canUseCstick helper documenting that C-stick is usable when single-button mode is off or the held item permits it.
Extended ftCommon_MotionVars with a fighterthrow sub-struct containing unknown fields plus victim, self_vel_y, and self_vel_x.
Retyped ftCommonData offsets around +7C8 and +7F4 from int/u8 padding to floats used by new comparisons and SFX timing.
Retyped SmashAttr x212C/x212D as u8 counters and typed Fighter_804D6528 as a table containing Vec2 shift data and count.
Moved ftCo_8008E5A4 declaration from a static forward declaration in ftCo_Damage.c to ftCo_Damage.h as an external void prototype.
The decomp bot reported one regression: ftAction_80073008 in main/melee/ft/ftaction dropped from 51.03% to 46.43%.
smart_moves: Matched a cluster of related ft_0D14 behavior by first improving supporting data layout: fighterthrow motion vars, SmashAttr counters, ftCommonData threshold fields, and Fighter_804D6528 table typing.
Factored repeated single-button/item C-stick gating into canUseCstick, making the recurring condition explicit while preserving small helper callsites.
Used previous-frame vs current-frame C-stick comparisons consistently to model edge-triggered input thresholds.
Kept address-based unknown function names where semantics were still uncertain, while adding concrete signatures and return types as evidence allowed.
Added lightweight semantic comments for gm_8016B0D4 and gm_8016B0FC, connecting opaque game-mode checks to friendly fire and single-button mode behavior.
Used exact float threshold fields from p_ftCommonData instead of hardcoded constants, which supports both matching and future field naming.
decomp_lessons: Large common-action files often require matching nearby structs and globals before the functions themselves become cleanly expressible.
When C-stick helpers compare input.cstick1 against input.cstick, they are usually edge detectors rather than simple held-input checks; preserve that distinction.
Single-button mode can change whether C-stick inputs are valid, and item state via it_8026B30C participates in that gating.
For smash-charge logic, table globals such as Fighter_804D6528 may encode both animation/shift vectors and a wrap count; typing them unlocks array indexing instead of raw UNK_T access.
Retyping data fields from integer/padding to float thresholds can be necessary for matching, especially in ftCommonData regions used by input predicates.
Partial progress is still valuable when isolated: ftCo_AppealS_IASA reached 98.55%, while other small helpers became full matches.
Header prototypes should be updated with precise return types once implementations prove them; examples here include float and Vec2* returns replacing UNK_RET/UNK_T.
Bot regressions in nearby unmatched functions should be called out explicitly; ftAction_80073008 regressed while enabling the new ftCo_800DEE84 signature.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions retain address-style names such as ftCo_800DEF38, ftCo_800DF50C, and fn_800DE798.
Common fighter action callbacks use ftCo_<Action>_<Callback> naming when action semantics are known, e.g. ftCo_ThrowB_Phys, ftCo_ThrownHi_Cam, ftCo_AppealS_Anim, and ftCo_AppealS_IASA.
Motion-variable union members use action-oriented lower-case names; this PR added mv.co.fighterthrow.
Partially understood struct fields keep offset-like names with type/semantic hints where available, such as x2124_frameSpeedMul and x2130_sfxBool.
Game-mode header comments can annotate opaque gm_8016Bxxx names with behavior, e.g. friendly fire enabled and single-button mode enabled.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK(0x18) in ftCo_800DF0D0, indicating stack padding was needed for the match.
Used ABS and SQ macros for C-stick magnitude/threshold tests rather than open-coded arithmetic.
Used p_ftCommonData float fields for threshold comparisons at x3C, xCC, xD4, xDC, xE0, x4B0, x7F4, x7F8, x7FC, x494, x98, attackhi3_stick_threshold_y, xB0, x31C, and x314.
Used typed u8 SmashAttr indices x212C/x212D for wrapping through Fighter_804D6528->x0 vector entries.
Preserved explicit zero comparisons such as gm_8016B0FC() == 0 and helper calls compared to 0 in the IASA chain, which may affect generated branch shape.
Introduced local temporaries and reordered extraction in ftAction_80073008 to match a new ftCo_800DEE84 call shape, but this exact function still regressed per bot report.
Used a nested block with redeclared Fighter/SmashAttr variables inside ftCo_800DEF38, likely to preserve original codegen around the charge-release transition.

review_feedback.summary: GALE01 report: matched code increased to 40.29% (+0.08%, +2956 bytes).
Reported 28 new full matches in main/melee/ft/ft_0D14, including ftCo_800DEF38, ftCo_800DF50C, ftCo_800DF0D0, ftCo_ThrowB_Phys, ftCo_ThrownHi_Cam, ftCo_AppealS_Anim, fn_800DE798, and ftCo_800DEE84.
Reported ftCo_AppealS_IASA improved from 0.00% to 98.55%, but not a full match.
Reported one regression in ftAction_80073008: 51.03% to 46.43%.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Check later PRs for a full match of ftCo_AppealS_IASA after this 98.55% partial match.
Search for follow-up fixes to ftAction_80073008 after the reported regression in PR 1873.
Look for later renames of Fighter_804D6528_t, ftCommonData x7C8/x7F4/x7F8/x7FC, and SmashAttr x2128/x212C/x212D.
Compare other ft_0D14 C-stick helper implementations for reuse of canUseCstick and previous/current cstick edge detection.
Search for uses of mv.co.fighterthrow to confirm or rename x0 and x4 fields.
